skill.name: legacy-modernizer
skill.description: Refactor legacy codebases, migrate outdated frameworks, and plan gradual modernization with backward compatibility.
skill.summary: Plan safe, incremental legacy modernization with tests, compatibility layers, and rollback steps.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported shell backticks and weak cryptography patterns, but review found only markdown text in SKILL.md. The backtick usage at line 26 is inline documentation, and the weak cryptography alerts at lines 3 and 28 are substring false positives in descriptive text. No executable code, network access, credential handling, or prompt injection attempt was found.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Finding: Markdown Inline Code
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged line 26 as Ruby or shell backtick execution. The line contains markdown inline code that names a playbook path, not executable code or command invocation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed line is a markdown bullet with inline code formatting. No shell command, script block, interpreter directive, or command execution API is present.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Finding: Weak Cryptography Text Match
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged line 3 as a weak cryptographic algorithm. The line is part of the skill description about refactoring legacy codebases and contains no cryptographic operation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is front matter prose, not code. No hash, cipher, key handling, or dependency reference appears on the line.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Finding: Weak Cryptography Text Match
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged line 28 as a weak cryptographic algorithm. The line describes the assistant role for modernization work and does not mention or use cryptography.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed text is a plain-language role statement. There is no algorithm selection, encryption code, or security-sensitive implementation detail.

content.value_statement: Legacy systems slow delivery and increase maintenance risk. This skill helps teams plan gradual upgrades with tests, compatibility layers, and rollback paths.
actual_capabilities: Create phased migration plans with milestones and rollback steps
Recommend tests before refactoring to preserve current behavior
Plan framework migrations such as jQuery to React or Python 2 to 3
Design adapter layers and compatibility shims for gradual change
Outline dependency update strategies that manage breaking changes
Prepare deprecation timelines for legacy APIs and workflows
limitations: Provides guidance and plans, not automatic migration execution
Requires project context to produce specific modernization steps
Cannot verify production behavior without tests or runtime access
Does not replace human review for breaking changes

use_cases.title: Frontend Framework Migration
use_cases.description: Plan a staged move from legacy JavaScript views to a modern component framework while preserving current pages.

use_cases.title: Backend Runtime Upgrade
use_cases.description: Prepare an upgrade plan for an older backend runtime with tests, dependency checks, and deployment safeguards.

use_cases.title: Technical Debt Reduction
use_cases.description: Prioritize risky legacy modules and define small refactoring phases that fit normal delivery work.

prompt_templates.title: Assess Legacy Risk
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner assessment of a legacy module or service.
prompt_templates.prompt: Assess this legacy system for modernization risk. Identify the highest-risk areas, missing tests, likely migration blockers, and the first three safe improvement steps.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Framework Migration
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate planning for replacing an old framework.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a phased migration plan from [current framework] to [target framework]. Include compatibility strategy, test coverage, rollout phases, and rollback procedures.

prompt_templates.title: Design Compatibility Layer
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an adapter or compatibility layer for this legacy interface. Preserve existing behavior, define new contracts, and list validation tests for each transition phase.

prompt_templates.title: Build Modernization Roadmap
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced roadmap for a multi-team modernization program.
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a modernization roadmap for this codebase. Include dependency sequencing, ownership, migration milestones, user impact, deprecation timelines, and release gates.

output_examples.input: Modernize a Python 2 web application with limited tests.
output: Start with characterization tests for critical user flows.
Upgrade tooling in a separate branch and keep a rollback path.
Port modules in small groups and validate behavior after each group.
Delay cleanup until the runtime upgrade is stable.

output_examples.input: Move a jQuery admin interface to React over several releases.
output: Add the new build pipeline beside the existing application.
Replace isolated widgets first, then larger page sections.
Use feature flags to control release exposure.
Remove old dependencies only after production validation.

output_examples.input: Reduce technical debt in a monolithic service.
output: Rank modules by change frequency, defect rate, and business impact.
Add tests around the highest-value workflows first.
Extract stable boundaries before changing internal implementation.
Track each phase with measurable delivery and reliability goals.

best_practices: Add tests that capture current behavior before refactoring legacy code.
Use incremental migration phases with clear rollback procedures.
Maintain backward compatibility until consumers have completed migration.
anti_patterns: Starting a full rewrite without a release-by-release migration path.
Removing legacy interfaces before consumers have moved to replacements.
Updating many major dependencies at once without isolation or tests.

faq.question: What does this skill help modernize?
faq.answer: It helps plan legacy code refactors, framework migrations, runtime upgrades, dependency updates, and compatibility strategies.

faq.question: Can it migrate code automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides plans, review guidance, and implementation steps that a developer must apply and verify.

faq.question: How should I start with a risky legacy system?
faq.answer: Start by documenting current behavior, adding tests for important flows, and choosing a small migration target.

faq.question: Does it support gradual migration?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill emphasizes phased rollout, compatibility layers, feature flags, and rollback procedures.

faq.question: Can it help with dependency updates?
faq.answer: Yes. It can sequence dependency updates, identify breaking-change risks, and define validation steps.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide the current stack, target stack, constraints, test coverage, deployment process, and known risky modules.
